title: [kimi-review] bc2213b: [SEVERITY: low] **Compiler portability**: `#pragma GCC diagn
description:
Commit: bc2213b8
Message: fix(hal): isolate upstream sign warning

Finding: [SEVERITY: low] **Compiler portability**: `#pragma GCC diagnostic` is honored by GCC and Clang (including armclang), but toolchains that don't recognize it will emit an "unknown pragma" warning — which can become a build failure under `-Werror`, the exact problem this commit is trying to solve. Since Zephyr supports multiple toolchains, consider guarding with `#if defined(__GNUC__) || defined(__clang__)`, or use Zephyr's toolchain abstraction (e.g. the `TOOLCHAIN_*` / `__diag_push`/`__diag_ignore`-style macros available in some Zephyr versions) for consistency.
